discussion_comments.comment_body: The FORCE flag seems powerful for recovery, but I'm also concerned about potential misuse—like creating PEL entries that don't correspond to actual stream data. How do you envision operators using this safely, and are there any guardrails (like validating that the stream ID still exists) you considered adding?

discussion_comments.comment_body: The FORCE path in xnackCommand validates that the stream entry actually exists before creating any PEL entry (t_stream.c:3837). If the provided ID doesn't correspond to a real message, the command silently skips it — no phantom PEL entry is created. This matches the behavior contract established by XCLAIM ... FORCE.

# Keep all of the Rust stuff in one place
 install-rust:
 ifeq ($(INSTALL_RUST_TOOLCHAIN),yes)
-	@RUST_VERSION=1.93.1; \
+	@RUST_VERSION=1.94.0; \
 	ARCH="$$(uname -m)"; \
 	if ldd --version 2>&1 | grep -q musl; then LIBC_TYPE="musl"; else LIBC_TYPE="gnu"; fi; \
 	echo "Detected architecture: $${ARCH} and libc: $${LIBC_TYPE}"; \
 	case "$${ARCH}" in \
 		'x86_64') \
 			if [ "$${LIBC_TYPE}" = "musl" ]; then \
 				RUST_INSTALLER="rust-$${RUST_VERSION}-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl"; \
-				RUST_SHA256="6a57ddfffa77bfa97ab325586b08fc1e96c3acb82ecc9f554cdb2ef748466ef2"; \
+				RUST_SHA256="57a78e193b11573da839c7177b8d29552aeb2c4751973179a384d472210f9c89"; \

discussion_comments.comment_body: This is not the correct checksum for the xz file. I think it is `9a358120ce1491a4d5b7f71a41e4e97b380b5db5d4ec31f7110f5b3090bd3d55`. Have a look at https://static.rust-lang.org/dist/channel-rust-1.94.0.toml and note that below in the code the .tar.xz file is being downloaded.
